issuesFound.title: missing page title
issuesFound.explanation: The `<title>` element is empty. This prevents screen reader users from identifying the page's purpose or distinguishing it from other open tabs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 7: `<title>Luminary Studio — Build accessible products faster</title>`

issuesFound.title: toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The "Solutions" button toggles a submenu but lacks an `aria-expanded` attribute. Users of assistive technology will not know the state of the associated content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 45: `<button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu">`

issuesFound.title: landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: There are multiple `<nav>` elements on this page, but this instance (containing account/CTA links) lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. Users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish between the primary and secondary navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 101: `<nav aria-label="Account">`

issuesFound.title: non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is non-descriptive when taken out of context. Screen reader users navigating by a list of links will not know what content the link leads to.
issuesFound.fix: Line 126: `Read more about our features (no credit card required)`

issuesFound.title: image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image is missing an `alt` attribute. While it is inside an `aria-hidden="true"` container, the `` element itself should still have an `alt` attribute (even if empty) to satisfy WCAG 1.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 141: ``

issuesFound.title: missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The "Annual" billing button is part of a selection mechanism for a billing period, but the pricing section lacks a way for users to identify the purpose of the inputs. More importantly, the email input in the newsletter section (Line 568) lacks an autocomplete attribute for a personal data field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: `<input type="email" id="newsletter-email" name="email" autocomplete="email" required aria-required="true" placeholder="you@example.com">`

issuesFound.title: table header missing scope
issuesFound.explanation: The column headers in the comparison table lack the `scope="col"` attribute. In a table with both row and column headers, this makes it difficult for screen readers to associate data cells with the correct headers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 464: `<th scope="col">Starter</th>`
Line 465: `<th scope="col">Pro</th>`
Line 466: `<th scope="col">Enterprise</th>`

issuesFound.title: form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The "First name" input is missing a proper association with its label. The label text is present, but it is not wrapped around the input nor does it use a `for` attribute that matches the input's `id`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 714: ``

issuesFound.title: missing legend
issuesFound.explanation: The `<fieldset>` used for interest checkboxes is missing a `<legend>`. This means the group of checkboxes has no accessible name or context for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: `<fieldset><legend>Areas of interest</legend>`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and lock scroll but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }` Line 1561: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch in the `pricing` module cycles between monthly and annual billing but does not update the `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked` state on the switch element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2321: `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) { const isYear = period === 'year'; toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear)); }`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error class and shows an error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3185: `} else { srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); errorEl.textContent = result.firstError; ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3179: `if (result.valid) { srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); errorEl.textContent = ''; ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the mobile menu trigger but does not initialise it with `aria-expanded="false"`. Assistive technologies may not communicate that the button controls expandable content until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1618: `_initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); _trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader.hide` function adds a CSS class to hide the header but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users navigating backwards may land on links in a header that is visually absent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1868: `function hide() { if (!_header) return; _hidden = true; addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); emit(_header, 'header:hide'); }`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler in the `productFilter` module switches the grid between list and grid modes. This significant layout change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2455: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(mode === 'list' ? 'Switched to list view' : 'Switched to grid view'); if (_gridEl) { ... }`

issuesFound.title: missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `toastManager` creates a dismiss button with the text content "×". Without a descriptive `aria-label`, screen reader users will only hear "times" or "button" and will not understand the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': 'Dismiss notification' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: missing label
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` creates a file list where the remove button for each file uses the character "✕" as its only content. This lacks an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3815: `const removeBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', class: 'upload-entry__remove', 'aria-label': `Remove ${entry.file.name}` });`

issuesFound.title: missing autocomplete on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form "Full name" input (selector `[name="full_name"]`) is missing the `autocomplete` attribute. This fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose for personal data fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3485: `const nameField = createElement('input', { type: 'text', name: 'full_name', autocomplete: 'name' }); (fix requires changes in external HTML or dynamic creation logic)`

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a click listener to the `zoneEl` (a `div`) and gives it `role="button"`, but it does not handle the Space key for activation, only Enter and Space are partially mentioned in a keydown handler that doesn't cover all standard button behaviors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3915: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and manage scroll locking but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }` Line 1573: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions for header dropdowns manage visibility via the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes but fail to update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button. Users of assistive technology will not perceive the change in state of the sub-menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: `function _openInstance(inst) { if (inst.open) return; inst.open = true; inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }` Line 1699: `function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { if (!inst.open) return; inst.open = false; inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function updates CSS classes on filter buttons but does not update the `aria-selected` attribute. Since these tabs are managed via a roving tabindex (tab pattern), the selected state must be programmatically exposed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2415: `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); btn.setAttribute('aria-selected', String(isActive)); });`

issuesFound.title: View mode toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler updates the `aria-pressed` attribute on all buttons in the group, but it uses a string comparison that may fail if attributes are not perfectly synced. It should explicitly set 'true' or 'false' based on the new state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2455: `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode)); });`

issuesFound.title: Pricing billing toggle does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function updates a CSS class on the toggle track but does not update the `aria-checked` attribute on the switch element (`_toggleEl`). Screen reader users will not know which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2915: `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) { const isYear = period === 'year'; toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear)); }`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `faqAccordion` animate the panel and update container classes but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing if an answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3315: `function open(item, announceToSR) { ... item.open = true; item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }` Line 3340: `function close(item, announceToSR) { ... item.open = false; item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Carousel play/pause button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` and `pause` functions for the testimonials carousel update the `aria-label` but do not update the `aria-pressed` state. A toggle button must communicate its state via the appropriate ARIA attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3605: `function play() { ... _playing = true; if (_playBtn) { _playBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true'); ... } }` Line 3616: `function pause() { ... _playing = false; if (_playBtn) { _playBtn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false'); ... } }`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` adds a CSS class and updates an error message but never sets the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not identify the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4145: `if (result.valid) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); } else { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); }`

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard panels do not update aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_goToStep` function uses the `hidden` attribute to hide inactive steps but also manually sets `aria-hidden="true"`. When a step is shown, `aria-hidden` is removed, but it should be explicitly set to "false" to ensure consistency in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4605: `leaving.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` Line 4611: `entering.panelEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region (Product Filtering)
issuesFound.explanation: When a user applies a filter in the product grid, the `applyFilters` function updates the visible cards and count badge but does not trigger a dedicated live region announcement. Users who cannot see the grid will not know how many products remain.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2335: `filterAnimation.run(toShowEls, toHideEls, _gridEl, () => { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory)); });`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region (Pricing Toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the prices on the page, but no live region announcement is made to confirm the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: `function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... const msg = period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly; liveRegion.announce(msg); ... }`

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region (Theme Switcher)
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher.set` function updates the document theme but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users may be unaware that the visual mode of the site has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5855: `function set(pref) { ... liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.themeChanged + ' ' + pref); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria-controls (Mobile Nav)
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but never establishes a programmatic relationship between them using `aria-controls`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1625: `_initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load (Mobile Nav)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger does not have its `aria-expanded` state initialised to "false" during the `init` function. ARIA states should be present before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1625: `_initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load (FAQ Accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: While the `faqAccordion.init` function sets `aria-expanded` for items that are open, it does not explicitly initialise it for closed items unless they already have the attribute in HTML.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3505: `faqRegistry.getAll().forEach(item => { setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', item.open ? 'true' : 'false'); ... });`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree (Search Suggestions)
issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer` shows and hides the listbox using the `hidden` attribute but does not update `aria-hidden`. Some screen readers may still perceive the container if `aria-hidden` is not synced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: `function show() { if (!_listEl) return; _listEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); }` Line 2095: `function hide() { if (!_listEl) return; _listEl.setAttribute('hidden', ''); _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on scroll-to-top button
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop` button is initialised from a selector but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label or text content). If the HTML is missing a label, the button is unusable for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: `_btn = qs(SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn); if (_btn && !_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) { _btn.setAttribute('aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop); }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support (Pricing Toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle `_toggleEl` is given `role="switch"` and `tabindex="0"` if it is not a button, but the code only attaches a `keydown` handler for Space and Enter. It does not handle the standard switch interaction (arrows) or ensure the role is fully supported.
issuesFound.fix: (The existing code at line 2995 handles Enter/Space, but the element should ideally be a native `<button>`). Line 2985: `if (_toggleEl.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { _toggleEl.setAttribute('role', 'switch'); _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form email input (`_emailEl`) is identified as a personal data field but does not have an `autocomplete` attribute assigned. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4385: `if (_emailEl) { _emailEl.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); inlineValidator.bind(_emailEl, ['required', 'email']); }`

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete on contact form fields
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form fields for "full_name" and "email" are personal data fields but are not assigned `autocomplete` attributes in the `init` function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4545: `if (field) { if (cfg.label.includes('email')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'email'); if (cfg.label.includes('name')) field.setAttribute('autocomplete', 'name'); inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules); }`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and lock scroll but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `function open(announceToSR) { if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); ... }` Line 1561: `function close(announceToSR) { if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); ... }`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch in the `pricing` module cycles between monthly and annual billing but does not update the `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked` state on the switch element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2321: `if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) { const isYear = period === 'year'; toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear); _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', String(isYear)); }`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module adds an error class and shows an error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3185: `} else { srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); errorEl.textContent = result.firstError; ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is corrected and becomes valid, the `_renderResult` function removes error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by some screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3179: `if (result.valid) { srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); errorEl.textContent = ''; ... }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the mobile menu trigger but does not initialise it with `aria-expanded="false"`. Assistive technologies may not communicate that the button controls expandable content until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1618: `_initialised = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'); _trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `stickyHeader.hide` function adds a CSS class to hide the header but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. Keyboard users navigating backwards may land on links in a header that is visually absent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1868: `function hide() { if (!_header) return; _hidden = true; addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden); _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); emit(_header, 'header:hide'); }`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` handler in the `productFilter` module switches the grid between list and grid modes. This significant layout change is not announced to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2455: `_productFilterState.viewMode = mode; liveRegion.announce(mode === 'list' ? 'Switched to list view' : 'Switched to grid view'); if (_gridEl) { ... }`

issuesFound.title: missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `toastManager` creates a dismiss button with the text content "×". Without a descriptive `aria-label`, screen reader users will only hear "times" or "button" and will not understand the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `const dismissBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', className: classes.dismissBtn, 'aria-label': 'Dismiss notification' }, '×');`

issuesFound.title: missing label
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` creates a file list where the remove button for each file uses the character "✕" as its only content. This lacks an accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3815: `const removeBtn = createElement('button', { type: 'button', class: 'upload-entry__remove', 'aria-label': `Remove ${entry.file.name}` });`

issuesFound.title: missing autocomplete on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form "Full name" input (selector `[name="full_name"]`) is missing the `autocomplete` attribute. This fails WCAG 2.1 SC 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose for personal data fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3485: `const nameField = createElement('input', { type: 'text', name: 'full_name', autocomplete: 'name' }); (fix requires changes in external HTML or dynamic creation logic)`

issuesFound.title: non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `fileUploadField` binds a click listener to the `zoneEl` (a `div`) and gives it `role="button"`, but it does not handle the Space key for activation, only Enter and Space are partially mentioned in a keydown handler that doesn't cover all standard button behaviors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3915: `zoneEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); inputEl.click(); } });`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open`, `mobileNav.close`, and `mobileNav.toggle` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified when the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1156: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` functions toggle CSS classes for visibility but do not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer when closed. This may leave the drawer's content discoverable by assistive technology even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1144: `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');` (inside open), Line 1155: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual cue

issuesFound.title: Transform transition on interactive elements lacks reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` component defines `transition: … transform var(--transition-fast);` which animates spatial movement, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disabling this transition, violating users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 720: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: .hero-image img transform transition without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 1245: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover transform without reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button on hover applies `transform: translateY(-1px);` with a transition, but no reduced‑motion override is provided, violating motion‑sensitivity preferences.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 1212: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()`, the dropdown panel is shown/hidden, yet the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never updated. This breaks the disclosure pattern required for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open action does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the answer panel and animates height, but only `close()` sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. The corresponding `aria-expanded="true"` is never applied, leaving the state ambiguous for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()`, after marking the item open, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`SELECTORS.billingToggle`) switches between monthly and annual pricing, but never updates `aria-pressed` to reflect its on/off state. Users of assistive tech cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix:
In `pricing._syncToggleState()` (or after `setPeriod()`), set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields with visual error classes and inserts error messages, but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the offending input.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions that open and close a dropdown (`_openInstance` / `_closeInstance`) change the panel’s visibility but never update the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` state, so assistive technologies cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for FAQ items change the panel’s hidden state but never set the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded`, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers missing `aria‑expanded` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is initialized the trigger receives `aria‑controls` but never gets an initial `aria‑expanded="false"`, so the accessibility tree is incomplete before any interaction.

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers missing `aria‑expanded` on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ parser sets up the button but does not initialise `aria‑expanded`, so the collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons receive a visual “active” class but never expose their state via `aria‑pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden without setting `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is closed the panel receives the `hidden` attribute but not `aria‑hidden="true"`, so the content may still be exposed to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels are hidden without setting `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: Closing an FAQ item only adds `hidden` to the panel; `aria‑hidden` is never set, leaving the content potentially reachable by AT.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter updates do not announce result count
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count badge is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users are unaware of how many items match.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does not announce to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/annual pricing updates the UI but never informs assistive technologies of the new billing period.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav hide does not set `aria‑hidden` on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed it receives a CSS class and `hidden` attribute, but `aria‑hidden` is never applied, so the off‑canvas menu may still be read by AT.

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav hide does not set `aria‑hidden` on drawer

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the dropdownMenu controller adds the “open” class and shows the panel, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot know the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria-expanded on close
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button, leaving the ARIA state stale.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` before hiding the panel.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open`, the FAQ item’s answer panel is shown, but the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`. Assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening an item.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` function marks fields visually but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to inputs that fail validation. Screen‑reader users receive no error indication.
issuesFound.fix: When a field is invalid, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` (or use `srUtils.markInvalid` which sets the attribute).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: After a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error styling but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute, leaving the field incorrectly announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: On successful validation, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` (or `srUtils.markValid`).

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod` function updates price displays but never announces the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”) to a live region. Users relying on screen readers are unaware of the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period switched to ' + period);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters` updates the product grid and count badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement of the new result count or active filters. Users cannot perceive filter results.
issuesFound.fix: After applying filters, update a hidden live region (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu’s `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions change visibility but do not announce the state change, leaving users unaware of the menu’s opening/closing.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing view‑mode toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (`data-view-mode`) toggle grid/list layout but do not set `aria-pressed` to indicate the active view, nor do they announce the change.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onViewToggle`, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` for the selected button and `false` for the other, and announce the new view via `liveRegion.announce`.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard’s “Previous”, “Next”, and “Submit” buttons are not given `aria-controls` or `aria-describedby` linking them to the current step panel, nor is the step change announced via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Add `aria-controls` pointing to the step panel IDs and after each step change call `liveRegion.announce('Step X of Y');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation submenu button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> used to expand/collapse mobile submenu sections lacks aria-expanded, so assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded/collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation panel toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes the off‑canvas mobile navigation panel does not have aria-expanded, leaving the panel’s state undisclosed to AT.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible — none of which hide content visually. issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator replaced with box-shadow on #search-input:focus

issuesFound.fix: Line 746: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator replaced with box-shadow on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline and rely solely on box-shadow for focus indication. This fails in High Contrast Mode and violates WCAG 2.4.7.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1466: .form-group input:focus, .form-group select:focus, .form-group textarea:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button has min-height and min-width set to 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size for touch interaction (WCAG 2.5.8).
issuesFound.fix: Line 652: .nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input

issuesFound.fix: Line 733: #search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 366: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)
issuesFound.explanation: The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, failing to suppress transitions and animations for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 426: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with transition: transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 505 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 505: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform: perspective() rotateY() rotateX() with transition on transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 869-873 is empty and does not disable this spatial motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience 3D rotation effects.
issuesFound.fix: Line 869: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions toggle CSS classes and scroll lock but fail to update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when the navigation menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1645: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1665: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer aria-hidden not managed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change visibility via CSS classes but do not toggle aria-hidden on the drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1645: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line 1665: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not notified when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1645: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line 1665: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button on page load. The initial state is not communicated to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1625: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not updated

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not initialised

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers do not identify the field as invalid.

issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description but does not remove or reset aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid to assistive technology after correction.

issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open() function sets the item state and animates the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close() function correctly updates it, creating an inconsistency.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus:not(:focus-visible) removes the outline for mouse users without providing a visible alternative. While this targets mouse focus, the rule removes the outline property entirely without replacement, which can confuse users who rely on visible focus indicators.
issuesFound.fix: Line 275: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: auto; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator in high-contrast mode. This is critical because Windows High Contrast Mode users rely on outline for visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280-282: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline and provides only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring on buttons. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 393: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator. Users navigating via keyboard cannot see when this button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 441: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and auto dimensions, which makes the content visible to sighted users but does not hide it visually; more critically, it fails to use the standard clipping technique (position: absolute, clip: rect(0,0,0,0)) needed to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. The current implementation renders content in the normal flow.
issuesFound.fix: Line 404-415: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, creating spatial movement on state changes without a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience dizziness or nausea.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform that creates a 3D perspective rotation effect on hover, but the empty prefers-reduced-motion media query block at lines 760-763 provides no override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 760-763: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 139: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or set to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses white text (#ffffff) on a blue background (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (16px font size).
issuesFound.fix: Line 541: Change background-color to a darker shade such as #3a6ea5 (or adjust --colour-brand-primary to #3a6ea5) to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast with white text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses white text (#ffffff) on a light red background (#ee9999), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 581: Change background-color to a darker shade such as #c53030 (or adjust --colour-error to #c53030) to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast with white text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on recommended plan badge
issuesFound.explanation: The "Most popular" badge uses white text (#ffffff) on the brand primary color (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for the small text (12px) used in the badge.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: Change background-color to a darker shade such as #3a6ea5 or increase font-size to 18px+ with bold weight to qualify for 3:1 large text threshold.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text underlines from all links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator in the default state. Links rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 for users with colour blindness or low vision who may not perceive the colour difference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 205: Add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; or text-decoration: underline; to provide a non-colour visual differentiator, or ensure links have sufficient font-weight or background differentiation from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as a non-zero outline, box-shadow, or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see any focus indicator on interactive elements, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius. No visible focus alternative is provided, preventing keyboard users from seeing which link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius. No replacement focus indicator is defined, violating focus visibility requirements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset. No visible alternative focus style is provided, making keyboard navigation invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The table wrapper sets outline: none with only outline-offset. As a focusable region (with tabindex), it requires a visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset. No visible focus alternative is provided, hiding the focused state from keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs set outline: none with outline-offset: 0. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement is provided, removing focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group select:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Select elements set outline: none with outline-offset: 0. No visible focus alternative is defined.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs set outline: none with only outline-offset. No visible focus alternative is provided for these custom-styled checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius. No visible focus indicator replacement exists.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius. Keyboard users cannot see which link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons require adequate touch targets for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are sized to width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a 28px touch target. While above the 24px minimum, this is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for some users to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually. More critically, it lacks the absolute positioning and clipping necessary to hide content visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree. This implementation fails to provide a screen-reader-only utility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower) and transform effects on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector (line 445) is empty, providing no override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the parallax-style transform animations.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have transition: transform var(--transition-fast) and .btn:active sets transform: translateY(1px). No reduced-motion override exists to disable this button press animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA primary button has transition: transform var(--transition-fast) and transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. No reduced-motion override is provided to disable this lift animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip links have transition: transform var(--transition-base) for their slide-in animation. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and may cause words to overlap at certain zoom levels.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) with color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.62:1, which is below the 4.5:1 required for normal text (12px font size at 600 weight). This makes the button text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5: --colour-brand-primary: #3a6ea5;
  (or alternatively change text color to #000000 for 7:1 contrast, or increase font-size to 18px+)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight. Links rely solely on colour (var(--colour-brand-primary)) to distinguish them from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer element's ID. Screen readers cannot establish the relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1249 (after _drawer assignment): Add `const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer does not trap focus
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab outside the drawer while it is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185 (inside open function): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` Line 1202 (inside close function): Add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the navigation state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1186 (inside open function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` Line 1203 (inside close function): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger lacks Enter/Space keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger keydown handler only handles the Escape key. If the toggle element is not a native button, Enter and Space keys do not activate it, preventing keyboard operation for users who rely on role="button" elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1222 (inside _onTriggerKeydown): Add `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(); break;` before the default case.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/year) do not update aria-pressed to indicate which period is selected. Screen readers cannot convey the active state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1915 (inside _periodBtns.forEach): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates the displayed prices but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1895 (inside setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` (and similar for annual/quarterly).

issuesFound.title: pricing comparison table expand not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing the comparison table does not announce the state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995 (inside setComparisonTableExpanded): Add `liveRegion.announce(expanded ? 'Comparison table expanded' : 'Comparison table collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the field is not marked with aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2385 (inside if (!result.valid) block): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` before `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid clear on valid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, aria-invalid is not reset to "false" or removed. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2390 (inside if (result.valid) block): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` before `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers lack Enter/Space handling
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers that are not native buttons (assigned role="button") do not respond to Enter or Space keys. The keyboard handler only manages arrow keys, Home, End, and Escape.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155 (inside _onKeydown switch): Add `case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(item); break;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion expand/collapse not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users do not receive feedback when accordion panels open or close.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120 (inside open function): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqOpened || '{title} answer expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));` Line 2140 (inside close function): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqClosed || '{title} answer collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not informed when menu panels appear or disappear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1345 (inside _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` Line 1358 (inside _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated result count and visible products are not announced. Screen reader users do not know that the product list has been updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1715 (inside filterAnimation.run callback): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} products').replace('{n}', totalMatches));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Toggling between grid and list view does not announce the new view mode to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1790 (inside _onViewToggle): Add `liveRegion.announce('Switched to ' + mode + ' view');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs aria-pressed not initialised
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons do not have aria-pressed set to reflect the active filter on page load. Only CSS classes are synchronised.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755 (inside _syncTabAriaStates): Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox aria-expanded not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The search input's aria-expanded attribute is not updated when the suggestion list opens or closes. Screen readers cannot track the visibility state of the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1565 (inside _open): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1580 (inside _close): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner appears, it is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that a dialog has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260 (after banner is appended to body): Add `liveRegion.announceAssertive(STRINGS.cookieTitle || 'Cookie consent dialog opened');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 852 (inside markInvalid): Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

fpTitles: mobileNav trigger lacks Enter/Space keyboard support
cookieConsent banner not announced
srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is never synchronized. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: After `inst.open = true;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: After `inst.open = false;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is shown and hidden dynamically, but the input element's aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the visibility state of the suggestions panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch._open: After `_searchState.open = true;`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line siteSearch._close: After `_searchState.open = false;`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons indicate active state via CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState: Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving stale invalid state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: After the aria-describedby cleanup block, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load, violating the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the initial state of the component.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the initial aria-expanded state is not set to "false" for closed dropdowns, leaving the trigger without a defined expanded state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation, the drawer is hidden via CSS classes but aria-hidden="true" is not set, leaving the closed drawer in the accessibility tree where screen readers may still access it.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Line mobileNav.open: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');` after opening.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown or hidden, no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the result count or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When switching billing periods, prices update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers about the new billing period or price changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse without announcing the state change or the content that was revealed to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} answer expanded`);`. Line faqAccordion.close: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.title} answer collapsed`);`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop activation not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll to top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top but no confirmation is announced to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop._onClick: After `window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: 'smooth' });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly but no live region announcement confirms the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line mobileNav.close: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing the state change to screen readers, making it difficult for users to know when the menu is available for interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');`. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: After `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: contactFirstName input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form first name input field lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from autofill and assistive technologies from understanding the field purpose programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line contactForm.init: After getting `_contactFirstName` element, add `setAttr(_contactFirstName, 'autocomplete', 'given-name');`.

issuesFound.title: contactLastName input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form last name input field lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing proper autofill and programmatic identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line contactForm.init: After getting `_contactLastName` element, add `setAttr(_contactLastName, 'autocomplete', 'family-name');`.

issuesFound.title: contactEmail input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email input field lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing email autofill and proper accessibility support.
issuesFound.fix: Line contactForm.init: After getting `_contactEmail` element, add `setAttr(_contactEmail, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: newsletterEmail input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing browser autofill and assistive technology recognition.
issuesFound.fix: Line newsletterForm.init: After getting `_emailEl` element, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

contactFirstName input missing autocomplete attribute
contactLastName input missing autocomplete attribute
contactEmail input missing autocomplete attribute
newsletterEmail input missing autocomplete attribute

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance() and _closeInstance(), but neither function updates aria-expanded on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether a dropdown menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line _closeInstance(): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the result count is updated visually via _updateCountBadge() but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology are not informed that the filter has been applied or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line applyFilters(): after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed (e.g., from monthly to annual), the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the prices have updated or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line setPeriod(): after `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);` in init (or at the end of setPeriod), add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line close(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when a dropdown menu expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Line _openInstance(): add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line _closeInstance(): add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function removes the open class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line close(): add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line open(): add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'false');` or `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop activation not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page to the top but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users are not informed that the page has scrolled back to the top.
issuesFound.fix: Line _onClick(): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after scrolling to top.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users are not informed when an answer is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line open(): add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} answer expanded`);`. Line close(): add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} answer collapsed`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users are left without focus context and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line close(): after `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.fix: Line: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.explanation: The button controls the mobile navigation panel using the isOpen state but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the menu is open or closed to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>

issuesFound.explanation: These buttons expand and collapse submenus using the isExpanded state but do not communicate their state to assistive technology.

issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of the FAQ answer panel using the isOpen state but lacks aria-expanded to convey the panel's state.

issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.explanation: The component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute, preventing screen readers from detecting invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.fix: Line: <textarea id={id} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button shows a loading state visually but lacks aria-busy to communicate this state to assistive technology. Screen readers do not announce that the button is busy.

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: StarRating inner spans not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star character spans lack aria-hidden, causing screen readers to read each star character individually. The rating value is already conveyed by the parent's aria-label.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
